docs.title: UNav Self-Serve Mapping Documentation
docs.text:
This site is for third-party partners onboarding a new indoor map into UNav.

Goal: start from raw 360 video and end with a navigation-ready map package.

docs.title: End-to-End Flow
docs.text:
1. Prepare hardware/software prerequisites.
  2. Collect 360 mapping video in the target space.
  3. Place raw input files in the required folder structure.
  4. Run mapping pipeline (python -m unav.run_mapper ...).
  5. Align SLAM map to floorplan (python -m unav.run_aligner ...).
  6. Label map entities with labelme (boundaries.json).
  7. Add multilingual labels with UNav translation GUI.
  8. Deliver final map artifacts under DATA_FINAL_ROOT.

docs.title: Who Should Use This
docs.text:
• Integrators onboarding a building/floor for the first time
  • Teams operating mapping as a repeatable field workflow
  • Engineers validating map readiness before API integration

docs.title: Read in This Order
docs.text:
1. Getting Started
  2. Data Collection SOP
  3. Input Output Contract
  4. Mapping Workflow
  5. Floorplan Alignment
  6. Map Labeling & Multi-language
  7. Troubleshooting

docs.title: Command Summary
docs.text:
# Mapping
python -m unav.run_mapper <data_temp_root> <data_final_root> <feature_model> <place> <building> <floor>

# Backward-compatible alias
python -m unav.run_mapping <data_temp_root> <data_final_root> <feature_model> <place> <building> <floor>

# Alignment
python -m unav.run_aligner <data_temp_root> <data_final_root> <place> <building> <floor>

docs.title: Floorplan Alignment
docs.text: This step creates transform_matrix.npy, required for mapping floorplan pixels and SLAM coordinates.

docs.title: Required Inputs
docs.text:
• <DATA_TEMP_ROOT>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/stella_vslam_dense/final_map.msg
  • <DATA_TEMP_ROOT>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/stella_vslam_dense/keyframes/
  • <DATA_TEMP_ROOT>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/stella_vslam_dense/eval_logs/keyframe_trajectory.txt
  • <DATA_FINAL_ROOT>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/floorplan.png
  • <DATA_FINAL_ROOT>/scale.json

docs.title: Run Command
docs.text:
python -m unav.run_aligner <data_temp_root> <data_final_root> <place> <building> <floor>

Example:

python -m unav.run_aligner \
  /mnt/data/UNav-IO/temp \
  /mnt/data/UNav-IO/data \
  New_York_City LightHouse 4_floor

docs.title: GUI Operation Steps
docs.text:
1. Select a keyframe from the left panel.
  2. Double-click keyframe panel and choose a stable visual feature.
  3. Double-click floorplan panel and place the corresponding 2D point.
  4. Repeat for multiple correspondences across floor (corners, junctions, doors).
  5. Keep adding correspondences until transform error is stable.
  6. Click Save Matrix.

docs.title: Minimum Correspondences
docs.text: At least 4 2D↔3D correspondences are required.

docs.title: Output
docs.text: • <DATA_FINAL_ROOT>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/transform_matrix.npy

docs.title: Quality Checks
docs.text:
• Correspondence error in GUI table should be low and consistent.
  • Projected trajectory should align with corridor layout on floorplan.
  • Re-open GUI and verify matrix reloads without visible drift.

docs.title: Data Collection SOP
docs.text: This section answers: "I am in a new indoor space. What exactly should I do with the camera?"

docs.title: Before Entering the Space
docs.text:
1. Charge camera batteries to full.
  2. Verify enough storage for full-floor recording.
  3. Clean all camera lenses.
  4. Prepare a floorplan image (for later alignment and labeling).
  5. Assign at least one assistant for door handling.

docs.title: Camera Settings
docs.text:
• Resolution: highest available (5K recommended)
  • Stabilization: on
  • Orientation: keep camera forward along walking direction
  • Motion: steady walk, avoid sudden spins

docs.title: Walking Procedure (Per Floor)
docs.text: Run at least 3 loops.

docs.title: Loop 1: Main Corridors (Loop Closure)
docs.text:
1. Walk major corridors and primary circulation paths.
  2. Keep movement continuous and form a closed loop if possible.
  3. Assistant opens doors ahead of time.

docs.title: Loop 2: Rooms and Side Areas (Coverage)
docs.text:
1. Enter each accessible room/branch area.
  2. Make a short pass inside, then return to corridor.
  3. Cover blind corners and dead ends.

docs.title: Loop 3: Door Interaction (Scene Diversity)
docs.text:
1. Start with doors closed where possible.
  2. Open/close doors while entering/leaving.
  3. Revisit key paths to capture appearance variation.

docs.title: Coverage Rules
docs.text:
• Every walkable public area must appear in video.
  • Slow down at narrow passages and door transitions.
  • Repeat problematic segments (dark, reflective, crowded).

docs.title: File Naming and Placement
docs.text:
Final mapping input must be placed as:

<DATA_TEMP_ROOT>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/<floor>.mp4

Example:

/mnt/data/UNav-IO/temp/New_York_City/LightHouse/4_floor/4_floor.mp4

If you recorded multiple loops separately, merge into one floor video before mapping, or keep one high-quality representative video named exactly <floor>.mp4.

docs.title: Scale Measurement for Alignment
docs.text:
You need meters-per-pixel for each floor.

  1. Mark at least 3 point pairs on floorplan.
  2. Measure real-world distances between those points.
  3. Compute average meters_per_pixel and store in scale.json.

Example:

{
  "New_York_City": {
    "LightHouse": {
      "4_floor": 0.0124
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Field QA Checklist (Before Leaving Site)
docs.text:
• Video file exists and can be played fully
  • Major corridors are covered
  • Rooms and side spaces are covered
  • Key doors captured at least once
  • Data copied to two storage locations

docs.title: Hardware Prerequisites
docs.text:
• 360 camera (5K recommended; e.g., GoPro MAX / Insta360 class)
  • Linux workstation with NVIDIA GPU (recommended for feature extraction/matching)
  • SSD storage with enough free space (mapping can generate many GB per floor)

docs.title: Software Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.8+
  • Docker (for stella_vslam_dense)
  • COLMAP on PATH (for triangulation)
  • labelme (for map annotation)

Install Python deps:

pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Build SLAM Docker Image
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/RoblabWh/stella_vslam_dense.git
cd stella_vslam_dense/docker
docker build -t stella_vslam_dense .

docs.title: Critical Runtime Files
docs.text:
Before running mapping, make sure these exist:

  • <DATA_TEMP_ROOT>/orb_vocab.fbow
  • <DATA_TEMP_ROOT>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/<floor>.mp4
  • <DATA_FINAL_ROOT>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/floorplan.png (needed before aligner step)
  • <DATA_FINAL_ROOT>/scale.json (needed before aligner step)

docs.title: Install and Launch Documentation Site Locally
docs.text:
pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material
mkdocs serve

Then open http://127.0.0.1:8000.

docs.title: Input Output Contract
docs.text: This is the exact folder contract expected by UNav mapping scripts.

docs.title: Parameter Definitions
docs.text:
Command format:

python -m unav.run_mapper <data_temp_root> <data_final_root> <feature_model> <place> <building> <floor>
  • data_temp_root: input raw map files + intermediate artifacts
  • data_final_root: final map artifacts for localization/navigation
  • feature_model: DinoV2Salad | MixVPR | CricaVPR | NetVlad | AnyLoc
  • place / building / floor: namespace identifiers

docs.title: Under data_temp_root
docs.text:
<data_temp_root>/
├── orb_vocab.fbow
└── <place>/<building>/<floor>/
    └── <floor>.mp4

docs.title: Under data_final_root (needed for aligner)
docs.text:
<data_final_root>/
├── scale.json
└── <place>/<building>/<floor>/
    └── floorplan.png

docs.title: Intermediate (data_temp_root)
docs.text:
<data_temp_root>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/
├── stella_vslam_dense/
│   ├── final_map.msg
│   ├── output_cloud.ply
│   ├── keyframes/
│   └── eval_logs/keyframe_trajectory.txt
├── perspectives/*.png
└── colmap_sfm/
    ├── sparse/0/cameras.txt
    ├── sparse/0/images.txt
    ├── pairs.txt
    ├── matches.h5
    └── database.db

docs.title: Final (data_final_root)
docs.text:
<data_final_root>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/
├── colmap_map/                  # triangulated map output
├── features/
│   ├── local_features.h5
│   └── global_features_<model>.h5
├── floorplan.png
├── transform_matrix.npy         # generated after aligner step
└── boundaries.json              # generated by labelme (map labeling step)

docs.title: Minimal Deliverable for Navigation
docs.text:
For each floor, ensure these files exist:

  • floorplan.png
  • boundaries.json
  • transform_matrix.npy

For multilingual labels, also provide:

  • _i18n/labels.json under data_final_root

docs.title: Map Labeling & Multi-language
docs.text:
This page covers:

  1. How to annotate boundaries.json with labelme
  2. How to run UNav translation GUI for multilingual labels

Reference examples:

  • https://github.com/ai4ce/UNav_Navigation/tree/main/example_data

docs.title: Part A: Map Labeling with Labelme
Output File
docs.text:
Save annotation file as:

<DATA_FINAL_ROOT>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/boundaries.json

docs.title: Group and Shape Rules (Code-Aligned)
docs.text:
The parser in unav/navigator/pathfinder.py expects the following:

group_id shape_type meaning required fields 0 polygon or rectangle walkable room/region label optional room name 1 polygon or rectangle obstacle (non-walkable) - 2 polygon or rectangle door area (added to walkable union) label optional door name 3 point normal navigation waypoint recommended label=waypoint 4 point inter-floor waypoint (stairs/elevator connector) label connector ID, description should be staircase or elevator 5 point destination label destination name, description orientation hint (left/right/center/up/...) 6 line companion line for group 4 waypoint label must match group 4 label

docs.title: Labelme Authoring Procedure
docs.text:
1. Open floorplan image in labelme.
  2. Draw walkable polygons (group_id=0).
  3. Draw obstacles (group_id=1).
  4. Draw doors (group_id=2).
  5. Place navigation waypoints (group_id=3).
  6. Place stair/elevator transfer points (group_id=4).
  7. Place destination points (group_id=5).
  8. Draw companion lines for each group 4 connector (group_id=6, same label).
  9. Save JSON as boundaries.json in floor folder.

docs.title: Inter-floor Connector Rule
docs.text:
To connect floors, group_id=4 labels must match across floors/buildings.

Example:

  • LH-e1 on floor 3 and LH-e1 on floor 4 means same elevator shaft
  • description=elevator or description=staircase controls cross-floor penalty logic

docs.title: Minimal JSON Example
docs.text:
{
  "shapes": [
    {"label": "corridor", "group_id": 0, "shape_type": "polygon", "points": [[0,0],[100,0],[100,20],[0,20]]},
    {"label": "pillar", "group_id": 1, "shape_type": "polygon", "points": [[40,5],[50,5],[50,15],[40,15]]},
    {"label": "door_A", "group_id": 2, "shape_type": "rectangle", "points": [[98,8],[104,12]]},
    {"label": "waypoint", "group_id": 3, "shape_type": "point", "points": [[20,10]]},
    {"label": "LH-e1", "group_id": 4, "shape_type": "point", "description": "elevator", "points": [[80,10]]},
    {"label": "Main Elevator", "group_id": 5, "shape_type": "point", "description": "up", "points": [[82,10]]},
    {"label": "LH-e1", "group_id": 6, "shape_type": "line", "points": [[80,6],[80,14]]}
  ]
}

docs.title: Part B: Multi-language Labels with UNav GUI
docs.text:
UNav translation editor writes labels to:

<DATA_FINAL_ROOT>/_i18n/labels.json

docs.title: Option 1: Use your wrapper in /home/unav/Desktop/unav-run
docs.text:
cd /home/unav/Desktop/unav-run
./run_translator.sh -r <DATA_FINAL_ROOT> -H 127.0.0.1 -p 5001

Then open:

http://127.0.0.1:5001

docs.title: Option 2: Run module directly from this repo
docs.text:
python -m unav.mapper.tools.i18n_label_web \
  --data-final-root <DATA_FINAL_ROOT> \
  --use-nav \
  --host 127.0.0.1 \
  --port 5001

docs.title: --use-nav vs file mode
docs.text:
• --use-nav: derives Place/Building/Floor/Destination tree from navigation assets (boundaries.json)
  • no --use-nav: falls back to scanning floor folders and optional destinations.json

Fallback destinations.json format:

[
  {"id": "101", "name": "Reception"},
  {"id": "102", "name": "Elevator"}
]

docs.title: labels.json Structure
docs.text:
{
  "places": {
    "New_York_City": {"en": "New York City", "zh-Hans": "纽约"}
  },
  "buildings": {
    "New_York_City/LightHouse": {"en": "LightHouse", "zh-Hans": "灯塔楼"}
  },
  "floors": {
    "New_York_City/LightHouse/4_floor": {"en": "4F", "zh-Hans": "四层"}
  },
  "destinations": {
    "New_York_City/LightHouse/4_floor/79": {"en": "Reception", "zh-Hans": "接待处"}
  },
  "aliases": {
    "zh-Hans": {
      "接待处": "New_York_City/LightHouse/4_floor/79"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Final Validation Checklist
docs.text:
• boundaries.json exists and loads without parse errors
  • All required group_id categories are present
  • group_id=4 labels match across floors where transitions should exist
  • _i18n/labels.json created and populated
  • Target language labels resolve correctly in app/localization flow

docs.title: Step 1: Prepare Paths and IDs
docs.text:
Example values:

DATA_TEMP_ROOT=/mnt/data/UNav-IO/temp
DATA_FINAL_ROOT=/mnt/data/UNav-IO/data
FEATURE_MODEL=DinoV2Salad
PLACE=New_York_City
BUILDING=LightHouse
FLOOR=4_floor

docs.title: Step 2: Verify Input File Contract
docs.text:
ls -lh "$DATA_TEMP_ROOT/orb_vocab.fbow"
ls -lh "$DATA_TEMP_ROOT/$PLACE/$BUILDING/$FLOOR/$FLOOR.mp4"

docs.title: Step 3: Run Mapping Pipeline
docs.text:
python -m unav.run_mapper \
  "$DATA_TEMP_ROOT" \
  "$DATA_FINAL_ROOT" \
  "$FEATURE_MODEL" \
  "$PLACE" \
  "$BUILDING" \
  "$FLOOR"

Backward-compatible alias:

python -m unav.run_mapping \
  "$DATA_TEMP_ROOT" \
  "$DATA_FINAL_ROOT" \
  "$FEATURE_MODEL" \
  "$PLACE" \
  "$BUILDING" \
  "$FLOOR"

docs.title: What Happens Internally
docs.text:
1. stella_vslam_dense runs in Docker on <floor>.mp4
  2. SLAM keyframes are sliced into perspective images
  3. Local/global features are extracted
  4. Image pairs are matched and geometrically verified
  5. COLMAP triangulates 3D map with known camera poses

docs.title: Stage 1 (SLAM)
docs.text:
Check:

ls "$DATA_TEMP_ROOT/$PLACE/$BUILDING/$FLOOR/stella_vslam_dense"

Expected key files:

  • final_map.msg
  • keyframes/
  • eval_logs/keyframe_trajectory.txt

docs.title: Stage 2 (Slicing)
docs.text:
Check:

ls "$DATA_TEMP_ROOT/$PLACE/$BUILDING/$FLOOR/perspectives" | head

Expected filenames:

  • 000123_pitch00_yaw05.png style pattern

docs.title: Stage 3 (Feature Extraction)
docs.text:
Check:

ls "$DATA_FINAL_ROOT/$PLACE/$BUILDING/$FLOOR/features"

Expected files:

  • local_features.h5
  • global_features_<feature_model>.h5

docs.title: Stage 4/5 (Matching + COLMAP)
docs.text:
Check:

ls "$DATA_TEMP_ROOT/$PLACE/$BUILDING/$FLOOR/colmap_sfm"
ls "$DATA_FINAL_ROOT/$PLACE/$BUILDING/$FLOOR/colmap_map"

Expected files:

  • pairs.txt, matches.h5, database.db
  • triangulation outputs under colmap_map

docs.title: Typical Runtime Failure Points
docs.text:
• Docker image stella_vslam_dense not built
  • Missing orb_vocab.fbow
  • Missing <floor>.mp4 or name mismatch
  • colmap command not found
  • Feature model checkpoint files not available under data_final_root/parameters/...

docs.title: Local Preview
docs.text:
pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material
mkdocs serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000.

docs.title: Build Static Site
docs.text:
mkdocs build

Output folder:

site/

docs.title: Deploy to GitHub Pages (Option A: mkdocs gh-deploy)
docs.text:
mkdocs gh-deploy

This pushes rendered docs to gh-pages branch.

docs.title: Deploy to GitHub Pages (Option B: GitHub Actions)
docs.text:
Use workflow file .github/workflows/docs.yml in this repo.

After pushing to main:

  1. Open repo Settings -> Pages
  2. Set source to GitHub Actions
  3. Each push to main publishes docs automatically

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: Usage printed or missing argument error.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
Provide all 6 positional args:

python -m unav.run_mapper <data_temp_root> <data_final_root> <feature_model> <place> <building> <floor>

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: stella_vslam_dense container exits or no SLAM outputs.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
• Build image stella_vslam_dense
  • Verify Docker GPU runtime works (docker run --gpus ...)
  • Check <DATA_TEMP_ROOT>/orb_vocab.fbow
  • Check <DATA_TEMP_ROOT>/<place>/<building>/<floor>/<floor>.mp4

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: colmap: command not found or triangulation error.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
• Install COLMAP and ensure it is on PATH
  • Confirm pairs.txt, matches.h5, database.db exist under colmap_sfm
  • Re-run mapping from start if feature files are incomplete

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: Error loading global descriptor checkpoint.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
The model loader uses data_final_root as parameter root. Verify checkpoint path exists under:

<DATA_FINAL_ROOT>/parameters/...

Example for DinoV2Salad:

<DATA_FINAL_ROOT>/parameters/DinoV2Salad/ckpts/dino_salad.ckpt

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: Transform computation error.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
• Add at least 4 valid correspondences
  • Ensure selected points are spread across map (not clustered)
  • Verify floorplan.png and trajectory/keyframes are from same floor

docs.title: Symptom
docs.text: No place/building/floor/destination listed.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
• With --use-nav: ensure boundaries.json exists per floor
  • Without --use-nav: provide destinations.json per floor
  • Check <DATA_FINAL_ROOT> path passed to GUI

docs.title: Port already in use for translation GUI
Symptom
docs.text: Cannot bind to requested port.

docs.title: Fix
docs.text:
• Use another port (-p 5002)
  • run_translator.sh already auto-fallbacks to next free port
